title: Cylinder Test
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fug58","text":"The Cylinder test is used to evaluate locomotor asymmetry in rodent models","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2mgfh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"kfik","text":"Glass cylinder 20 cm in diameter 30 cm high","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"90j4j","text":"Angled mirror","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ccpv7","text":"70% ethanol for cleaning between each session","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Place the mouse in the transparent cylinder for 00:05:00
Place an angled mirror at the back of the cylinder to allow observation of behavior from every angle
Record mouse behavior for 00:05:00 Main outcome measures: Left/Right contacts, Number of rears, L/R hind-paw contacts, Total time rearing, Time on the ground
creator: Jacquelyn Haytayan
